brief_notes: Well-written bioinformatics tool paper introducing sRNAfrag for biotype-agnostic sRNA fragmentation analysis. Strong benchmarking against known miRNA biology (93.7% sensitivity, 97.7% precision) provides solid validation. The multi-mapping analysis recovering 91% of seed sequences vs 52% with unique mappers is a compelling result. Cross-species snoRNA conservation analysis across 4 eukaryotes adds biological novelty. Strengths: clear pipeline description, quantitative benchmarks, practical utility demonstrated. Weaknesses: no figures/workflow diagrams, limited discussion of computational performance/scalability, conservation analysis relies solely on sequence homology without functional validation, no comparison to other tools on the same datasets, some method details are sparse (e.g., coverage threshold parameters).
